strengths: Deep, production-proven TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple Berlin-based companies directly satisfies the role's primary technical requirement, with React experience at Optilyz further confirming fullstack coverage.

Current role at re:cinq involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP integrations, covering the LLM/API nice-to-have outright; GISCollective's Kubernetes deployment and Optilyz's AWS CDK usage address two more nice-to-haves simultaneously.

Sustained practice of TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source library authorship (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) demonstrates the clean architecture mindset the job description explicitly calls out.
concerns: Candidate holds Staff and Senior titles with roughly 15 years of continuous professional experience; the role targets Junior/Mid level, which may create expectation or compensation misalignment.
PostgreSQL is not mentioned by name; relational DB exposure appears through SQL and MySQL references only, requiring verification of comfort with PostgreSQL-specific features.
Nest.js is absent from the resume; backend framework experience centres on custom Node.js patterns and non-NestJS stacks, meaning some ramp-up may be needed for that specific framework.
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo enters this screening with the full technical surface area the role demands — TypeScript, React, Node.js REST APIs, and relational databases — plus substantial coverage of the nice-to-have list that most candidates at this level would lack entirely, including live LLM/MCP integration work and Kubernetes deployment experience. His record of TDD, clean architecture, and knowledge-sharing aligns directly with the team culture the job description signals. The primary uncertainty is not technical fit but whether the role's Junior/Mid framing and its scope of responsibility match what a Staff-level engineer is looking for; if the candidate is deliberately seeking a focused IC position in a domain he finds meaningful, the technical ceiling concern evaporates and he represents a high-upside hire.
